questions.question: Most natural way to book:
options: I'd like to book a table, please
questions.explanation: 'I'd like to...' est poli et naturel.

questions.question: 'Do you have anything available?' means:
questions.explanation: Available = disponible.

questions.question: Most polite way to order:
options: Could I have the fish, please?
questions.explanation: 'Could I have...please?' = la plus polie.

questions.question: 'Medium rare' means:
questions.explanation: Medium rare = à point, légèrement rosé au centre.

questions.question: 'Is everything alright?' during a meal is:
questions.explanation: Politesse standard. Réponse : 'Lovely, thanks!'

questions.question: 'GF' on a menu means:
questions.explanation: GF = Gluten-Free. V = Vegetarian. Ve = Vegan.

questions.question: 'Does this contain nuts?' asks about:
options: La présence de fruits à coque
questions.explanation: Nuts = fruits à coque. Question de sécurité alimentaire.

questions.question: 'Cross-contamination' matters because:
questions.explanation: Contamination croisée = traces d'allergènes via ustensiles/surfaces.

questions.question: At a pub, to order you must:
questions.explanation: Au pub = PAS de service à table. Tu vas au bar.

questions.question: Someone bought you a pint. You should:
questions.explanation: Le système des rounds est sacré. Tu offres la suivante.

questions.question: 'Crisps' in British English are:
questions.explanation: Crisps (UK) = chips en sachet. Chips (UK) = frites !

questions.question: 'A pint' is:
questions.explanation: A pint = 568ml. Plus qu'un demi français.

questions.question: 'Last orders!' means:
questions.explanation: Last orders = annoncé ~20min avant la fermeture du bar.

questions.question: Past simple of 'go':
questions.explanation: Go → went (past simple). Gone = participe passé.

questions.question: 'Did you go to the pub?' — correct because:
questions.explanation: Avec 'did', le verbe revient à sa forme de base : did you GO.

questions.question: 'Bought' is the past of:
questions.explanation: Buy → bought. Bring → brought. Build → built.

questions.question: 'On tap' means:
questions.explanation: On tap = à la pression / au fût.

questions.question: 'My round' means:
questions.explanation: My round = c'est ma tournée. Tu paies pour tout le groupe.

questions.question: 'Lager' is:
questions.explanation: Lager = blonde. Ale = ambrée. Bitter = amère.

questions.question: 'Though' at the end of a sentence means:
questions.explanation: 'Though' en fin de phrase = quand même / cependant. Très britannique.
